column1: # Corpus cases a target skips in the differential comparison. One entry per line; blank lines and
# lines beginning with `#` are ignored. An entry is either `target<TAB>feature` (the whole
# `corpus/ast/` subdirectory) or `target<TAB>feature/case` (a single case stem within it, for when
# only one case in an otherwise-passing directory is skipped). Both the Rust golden-writer test and
# the shell conformance suite read this file and skip the listed cases, reporting the skip count.
# Most entries mark a writer path not yet implemented — delete the line when it lands and the corpus
# case activates automatically. A few mark an intentional divergence in the differential comparison,
# where a byte-for-byte match would demand wrong output or the difference is a negligible cosmetic
# artifact; those are documented under the relevant format in docs/STATUS.md and are expected to stay.
# - image-external: an absolute image URL is emitted verbatim rather than resolved against the
#   chapter directory; prefixing a chapter-relative `../` onto it would yield a broken
#   `../https://…` path, so the case is not compared.
# - raw-latex-block: a raw LaTeX block has no XHTML representation and is dropped, leaving no stray
#   whitespace where it stood — a negligible cosmetic difference not worth comparing.
# - literal-control-chars: characters the XML specification forbids are stripped from the text, since
#   an XHTML document that contains them is not well-formed and cannot be opened; a byte comparison
#   is meaningless when leaving them in produces an archive no reading system can parse.
# An intra-document link (a target of the form `#id`) is not yet rewritten to each format's own
# cross-reference form: Beamer wants `\hyperlink{id}{…}`, reveal.js prefixes the fragment with `#/`,
# and Typst references a `<label>`. The generic `#id` these writers emit lands elsewhere; the case
# activates for each once its internal-link path does.
# Block content (a list) inside an otherwise-empty table cell is not yet rendered at parity by these
# writers, which flatten or misplace the cell's paragraphs and bullets.
# The man writer's line fill breaks a long run of adjacent inline equations at a column that depends
# on the run's width, so these wide math paragraphs wrap at points not worth comparing; content is the same.
# A code block whose body already ends in a newline gains a spurious extra blank line (a stray
# trailing break in DOCX) from these writers; the trailing-terminator handling is only at parity in
# A table nested inside a block quote is not yet reflowed at parity by these writers, which drop the
# quote's blank continuation line or misprefix the table rows with the quote marker.
# A list item whose first child is itself a block — a nested list with no leading paragraph — is
# spaced differently by these writers (a stray or missing blank line, a dropped tightness marker, or
# a missing leading placeholder), so the nested-first-block layout is skipped until it is uniform.
# A bullet list nested inside a definition-list item is not yet laid out at parity by these writers —
# a missing tightness marker, list-continuation glue, or definition-term prefix leaves the inner
# bullets on their own lines — so the case is skipped.
# A bullet list nested directly inside an ordered-list item gains a stray blank line from the Typst
# writer, so this mixed-nesting case is skipped.
# A table caption carried on a wrapping div, rather than in the table's own caption slot, is not yet
# lifted back out by these writers, so the caption text is dropped; the case is skipped until that
# A table whose header row is entirely empty is still emitted with that header by these writers (an
# empty header row, a `thead` of blank cells, or a `header` option), where suppressing a contentless
# header is a not-yet-implemented refinement; the empty-header case is skipped.
# An image whose source is an inline `data:` URI is emitted verbatim by these writers — placed
# directly in the graphics command (LaTeX, Beamer), the image path (Typst), or the `img` source
# (EPUB) — rather than decoded into the target's own embedded form (an extracted media file or an
# inline byte array); extracting embedded images per target is not yet implemented, so these cases
# A hard line break inside a table cell is not yet rendered at parity by the DokuWiki writer, which
# doubles the break escape and widens the column to the unsplit content; the case is skipped until
# Intentional ODT math divergences (see docs/STATUS.md → ODT): every formula embeds as a MathML object
# part, except two cases whose exact shape the shared math parse tree cannot carry:
# - frac-styles: `\dfrac`/`\tfrac` collapse to the same fraction node, so the display-style wrapper
#   that distinguishes them cannot be reconstructed.
# - modulo: the infix `\bmod`/`\mod` operand is a following sibling, not a captured argument, so it
#   sits beside the operator group rather than inside it.
